channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Iran reviews US peace proposal as war diplomacy intensifies
item.description: Iran is reviewing a US proposal to formally end the war, though key demands on Irans nuclear program and reopening the Strait of Hormuz remain unresolved. The development comes as Irans foreign minister meets Chinas top diplomat in Beijing ahead of Trump-Xi summit.

item.title: Hantavirus cruise ship heads to Canary Islands amid health emergency
item.description: A luxury cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak departed for Spains Canary Islands after 3 passengers were evacuated. Four people have died from the outbreak, with at least 8 confirmed infections aboard the Dutch-flagged MV Hondius.

item.title: Vatican cardinal calls Trump attacks on Pope Leo strange before Rubio visit
item.description: A senior Vatican cardinal criticized President Trumps public attacks on Pope Leo over the Iran war as strange, one day before US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is scheduled to meet with the pontiff. Tensions between Washington and the Vatican have escalated over differing approaches to the Iran conflict.

item.title: France Deploys Aircraft Carrier to Red Sea for Potential Hormuz Mission
item.description: France moved its carrier strike group to the Red Sea as part of planning for a possible mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz, urging Washington and Tehran to consider a diplomatic proposal given the global economic impact of their competing blockades.

item.title: Pope Leo to Visit Canary Islands on Spain Trip, Spotlight on Migrant Crisis
item.description: Pope Leo will visit the Canary Islands, a major entry point for migrants crossing from Africa, as part of a tour of Spain next month. The Vatican highlighted the islands as a destination for desperate migrants braving dangerous Atlantic waters.

item.title: Euro Zone Services Activity Contracts for First Time in Nearly a Year
item.description: Euro zone services activity slumped in April, contracting for the first time in almost a year as weakening demand and the Middle East war weighed on consumer-facing sectors. The deterioration adds to concerns about Europe economic resilience.

item.title: Germany Weighs Bigger Stake to Block Italian Takeover of Commerzbank
item.description: German government members are examining a drastic step to take a bigger stake in Commerzbank to fend off UniCredit unwanted bid, viewing the lender as strategic. The move reflects growing European banking tensions.

item.title: US-Iran Deal Framework Emerging After Trump Pauses Hormuz Operation
item.description: The US and Iran are moving toward a negotiation framework after Trump halted a maritime escort mission in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran is reviewing a US proposal while key sticking points over uranium enrichment remain unresolved.

item.title: Modi BJP Seizes Historic Control of West Bengal, Extending Grip to 70% of Indian States
item.description: Narendra Modi ruling coalition now controls roughly 70% of India state legislatures after BJP stunning conquest of West Bengal, ending three consecutive terms of Mamata Banerjee Trinamool Congress. The victory represents the conquest of the party final frontier.

item.title: Israel Strikes Beirut for First Time Since Ceasefire, Targeting Hezbollah Commander
item.description: An Israeli airstrike hit Beirut southern suburbs targeting a Hezbollah Radwan Force commander, marking the first attack on the Lebanese capital since the ceasefire was brokered in mid-April. The strike comes as US-Iran negotiations appear to be progressing.

item.title: US Navy Disables Iranian Oil Tanker in Gulf of Oman
item.description: US military disabled an Iranian-flagged oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman on Wednesday for allegedly violating the blockade of Iranian ports, marking continued tensions even as diplomatic talks progress.

item.title: Central Banks Pause Rate Cuts as Iran War Roils Markets
item.description: The Iran conflict has forced major central banks to hold interest rates steady in April, disrupting global monetary easing efforts amid inflation pressures and market volatility.

item.title: Pussy Riot Protesters Swarm Russia's Venice Biennale Pavilion
item.description: Activists from Pussy Riot and FEMEN staged a protest against Russia's presence at the Venice Biennale, releasing colored smoke and swarming the pavilion in pink balaclavas.

item.title: FBI Searches Virginia Senate Leader's Office in Corruption Probe
item.description: The FBI executed a search warrant at Virginia Senate Leader Louise Lucas's office as part of an ongoing corruption investigation.

item.title: Trump says Iran wants to negotiate as both sides edge toward peace deal
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Iran wants to negotiate and make a deal, saying U.S. operations in Iran were going smoothly. The statement comes as sources indicate both sides are closing in on a memorandum to end the war.

item.title: Iran reviewing fresh U.S. proposal as memorandum to end war takes shape
item.description: Iran said Wednesday it is reviewing a new U.S. proposal, with sources indicating Washington and Tehran are moving closer to a one-page memorandum to end the Gulf conflict while leaving complex issues like Iran nuclear program for later.

item.title: U.S. NATO exit would force Europe into fiscal creativity and faster defense spending
item.description: A Reuters commentary examines how a potential U.S. withdrawal from NATO would require Europe to spend more and faster than the $350 billion already pledged by 2035, suggesting low-cost tech adoption and new mutual financial schemes.

item.title: Poland warns Russia shifting from low-cost recruits to professional sabotage cells
item.description: Poland raised alarms about Russia transitioning from using low-cost recruits to deploying professional sabotage cells, following what the prime minister called an unprecedented act of sabotage with explosions in November 2025.

item.title: China Advances Submarine Detection Tech with Quantum SQUID Sensors
item.description: Chinese scientists report progress on gravity-detecting SQUID sensors capable of identifying US nuclear submarines by their mass signature, potentially threatening US undersea supremacy.

item.title: Eurovision Crisis Deepens Over Israel-Gaza Controversy
item.description: Multiple countries are boycotting or threatening to withdraw from Eurovision 2026 over Israel participation, with artists including Massive Attack and Kneecap calling for fans to boycott the contest.

item.title: US and Iran Near Deal on Uranium Transfer
item.description: Reports indicate Washington and Tehran are closing in on an agreement that would see Iran give away its enriched uranium stockpile as part of a broader peace framework.

item.title: Vatican Cardinal Calls Trump's Attacks on Pope Leo 'Strange'
item.description: A senior Vatican cardinal criticized President Trump's disparaging comments about Pope Leo over the Iran war as 'strange,' ahead of Secretary of State Marco Rubio's scheduled talks with the pope. The rare public rebuke highlights tensions between Washington and the Holy See.

item.title: Japan Conducts First Overseas Offensive Missile Test in Philippines
item.description: Japan's Self-Defense Forces fired a Type 88 anti-ship missile during a joint exercise with US, Australian, and Philippine forces, hitting a decommissioned ship in waters facing the South China Sea. The drill marks Japan's first overseas offensive missile test since WWII and signals Tokyo's deepening security partnerships in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: Russia to Buy Foreign Currency as Iran War Oil Windfall Boosts Revenues
item.description: Russia will purchase foreign currency on the market for the first time since the Ukraine war began, as surging oil prices from the Iran conflict have boosted Moscow's crude export revenues. The Finance Ministry announcement signals a shift in Russia's fiscal position driven by Middle East tensions.

item.title: Gulf's Trade Lifeline Hangs on Two UAE Ports Amid Hormuz Crisis
item.description: The UAE ports of Fujairah and Khor Fakkan have become critical chokepoints for Gulf trade as the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked. Grain offloading and crude loading continue at Fujairah while regional supply chains reroute through the eastern UAE facilities.
